subject: Mathematics
style: Uganda flag-inspired classroom infographic; exact SVG text overlay; no generated text inside raster image.
title: 01 Sets (Set Concepts)
details: Finite Set: Has a countable number of members.
Representing finite sets with all members.
evidence_excerpts: Concrete exploration of countable objects (stones) vs. uncountable quantities (sand). The learner identifies sets that have a limited number of elements versus those that do not. Practical counting of stones.; Attempting to count grains of sand.; Identifying which count can be finished.; Group activity with local materials.

Definition of Finite (limited members) and Infinite (unlimited members) sets. The learner defines finite and infinite sets correctly. Finite Set: Has a countable number of members.; Infinite Set: Has members that go on forever.; Examples from daily life.

Pictorial representation of sets using Venn diagrams and member listing. The learner represents finite and infinite sets using diagrams. Representing finite sets with all members.; Representing infinite sets with patterns.; Using circles to group elements.
